reviews.review_description: Dont buy this net doesnt move when you hit the shuttle it just doesnt register most of the time not worth your money!

reviews.review_title: Game is always being struck in middle
reviews.review_description:
Wastage of money - cant play online 
Can only play with AI which is wastage. 

Not sure how can i get refund for this

My first impression… The AI avatar moves like a puppet missing its strings — no hands, just a floating head wobbling around a racket. The Head and strokes have zero link to the shot that follows, so forget about reading or anticipating anything. No clue how it behaves online yet — I might try, if I ever stop shaking my head first.

The graphics and atmosphere are as lively as a funeral. The net is a ruler-straight line, doesn’t sag in the middle, and when a shuttle hits it, it doesn't even blink. The icons are thick enough to serve as a runway for airplanes , and the sound — once you kill the background music (which you will) — is about as inspiring as playing on the moon.

Overheads work fine as long as you only return a friendly handshake, don't try to smash it to the ground, it will always fly behind the backline. Somehow the timing feels a bit off, a bit late, compared to RL. Anything around body height turns into some kind of badminton parody. The scoring sits low in the corner like it’s hiding from the embarrassment.

Okay, credit where it’s due: you can adjust the grip and use AR to see your room boundaries — both standard features missing in Badminton Time. That’s the reason I tried this one.

But Badminton Time at least feels alive — even with the same three sound effects looping like running on a cracked vinyl disk. It’s more arcade, sure, but the shots actually make sense. The only real flaw is that it won’t let you rotate the grip, so reverse shots need a grip worthy of a kid, not a real player.

This one though… dead. The birdie drops like a brick, no rebound on the floor, no spinning, the net doesn’t twitch, and the ambiente feels like someone unplugged it. It’s all stiff, sterile, and joyless.

And worst of all — it doesn’t even follow badminton rules. You can hit the shuttle two or three times before it crosses the net. At that point, it’s not badminton — it's juggling and teasing.

reviews.review_title: Best Badminton VR Game So Far
reviews.review_description:
Best Badminton VR Game So Far, but Some Areas Need Improvement

I’ve been playing badminton since I was nine years old, so this review comes from the perspective of a real-life intermediate/advanced player. If you’ve never played badminton before, your experience might differ.

I mainly use this game for "shadow badminton" training at home when I can't access a real court.


Game Modes

The game has two main modes:

1. Match Mode – Play a standard match, either against an AI or online against other players.


2. Training Mode – This mode is quite useful because you can place a "ball machine" on the other side of the court. You can set its position and adjust where the shuttle lands on your side. You can also create sequences, mimicking real-life training drills.


However, adjusting the machine and shot placements could be more user-friendly.


UI Feedback

The UI could use some improvements:

1. Opening the menu does not pause the game.


2. There is no clear way to exit an online match if the opponent disconnects. I have to restart the game to continue playing.


3. Sometimes, button presses don’t register immediately, requiring multiple attempts.



Gameplay Experience

The gameplay feels very good, especially the shuttle’s trajectory. However, since a real racket isn't being used, certain shots can feel inconsistent. I’ve tried adjusting my grip on the controller to compensate for forehand and backhand shots.

Here are a few areas that could be improved:

1. Overhead backhand shots are difficult to execute – The racket often misses the shuttle, possibly due to movement tracking.


2. Drives don’t seem to work properly – My attempts usually result in weak shots or the shuttle going into the net.


3. Smash mechanics feel inconsistent – I haven’t been able to perform smashes consistently. Many attempts result in clears that go out of bounds.


4. Net shots require too much force – They feel harder to control compared to real badminton.


5. AI sometimes returns serves incorrectly – The AI returns serves to the wrong side when it should let the shuttle drop. I’m not sure if this happens in online matches as well.


6. Illegal serves are possible – The game allows throwing the shuttle up and serving from above, similar to tennis, which isn’t allowed in badminton.


7. AI movement could be improved – At times, the AI avatar moves in ways that don’t seem natural, making certain shots appear difficult to predict.



Final Thoughts

This is the best badminton VR game I’ve played so far, but refining the UI and improving shot mechanics would make it even better. If these areas are addressed, it could provide an even more realistic and enjoyable experience.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the great feedback, we will improve the game accordingly.

reviews.review_description: I've played real badminton for 20 years. This game requiers a lot of improvements. It's nice to play, but it's still a toy, but nothing wrong if it's an only toy. There are some more serious problem though.I have many problems to play smash and cross drop shots. A lot of shuttlerocks fly too far even though I didn't mention to play so strong. I also think shuttlerocks fly faster than in real world. However I hope next updates will improve some physics issues. This game has a big pottential and  it's worth to improve.

This is a good game and feels quite similar to the real thing. The movement of the shuttlecock is very accurate and the longer overhead shots are very responsive. The main area for improvement would be the short shorts and the net play. Drop shots in real badminton require almost no swing, but this isn't quite replicated accurately. Hopefully the team can continue to work on this as the game has a lot of potential. It does require a lot of room to get the most out of it but this is to be expected.

I play this a lot!  I have played online against a real opponent and enjoyed the experience but mostly play against the disembodied avatar. My only complaint is that the racket changes length or moves on its own at the most inconvenient times. For an enhancement, even in intermediate mode, service rules should apply. Also as long as the shuttle doesn’t hit the ground, it can be hit repeatedly to get it over the net

Overall the game is okay. The physics in general are quite good, although for delicate shots like net shots and drop shots it’s not very good at all. If you try to play a net shot the shuttle just dies, you end up having to hit it quite hard which is not at all like a real shuttle. Besides that the UI is also a bit janky but overall okay

Physics top, Music top and the AI seems to learn from the player, even in easy mode the games are longer with more balls-exchanges, great! Only Advise: The graphics of the environment should be better to have a more immersive feeling! Go ahead, worth every cent!

Game has potential. 
Physics very good. 
Graphics definitely need to be improved(look at eleven table tennis or first person tennis) it's out of date. 
Also need implement some teleport options for small rooms(again check how it works in first person tennis). 
Cost is to high at current moment but I hope devs will work on graphics improvements because it can be the best badminton game.

reviews.review_title: This is the best app period
reviews.review_description: I’ve had every VR headset since the oculus developer kit one and I have been searching for a badminton simulator that felt good to play and that I could actually run around without movement assist. This is that game and this is perfect. Everything feels great. It makes every cent I have spent on virtual reality worth it now that this game is installed on my headset. The mixed reality pass is perfect for me not running into furniture also I’m so glad that’s included because I definitely would’ve have smashed a lamp otherwise. 10/5 stars

developer_response.body: Thanks very much for your comments, we will continue to improve the game.

reviews.review_description: Great realistic badminton game.  Love it that you can select area of movement in settings for different size area.  The setting panel pop up needs to be fixed so hard to get it on and off.  Did dev add a leaderboard or is that fake? Hard to find friend so wish just can see random people online and play against them

Very good in terms of the shuttlecock feeling like an actual one and not feeling too light or it floating randomly like i have seen in other vr games

But a few small things which would make this game even more perfect:
1)The ai has no variation and the shots are always aimed at the same spots over and over again which can become a bit boring also  even the easy ai returns all shots and its really hard to score

2)If teleport mode or allowing us to move with thumbstick can be added would be amazing as a lot of us want to play full court but dont have the space

3)Please fix the menu as it feels buggy and when we try to open menu the ai keeps playing and serving,also the menu should be hidden in the middle of a game for a cleaner look 

4)Add option to decrease net height in game,its kind of hard to get it over sometimes while doing a smash

5)This is personal preference but i would love if the shuttlecock had more colours and not just red as its kind of hard to see sometimes,maybe yellow or green would be better, not sure about white.
6)Add option to turn off the music for more realistic focus on effects

reviews.review_title: ONE AND ONLY TRUE BADMINTON GAME
reviews.review_description:
This is THE one and only WORKING badminton multiplayer game on Quest store. The physics is PERFECT. 

PLEASE ADD DOUBLES MATCHES!!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot, we will do the doubles Matches soon.

reviews.review_description: Although this game is in the development stage, but can not deny that this is a good completion of the game!

The physics feel pretty good but it doesn’t look the best. The menu is also kinda bad, it looks bad and functions okay. Training mode also doesn’t work with left handed. Locomotion would be good. One thing with the ai is that it randomly teleport and flickers between different states which can be confusing!

As an experienced badminton player I thought this was very good and fairly easy to configure in a medium sized room. Would be interesting to play in a larger area. Not sure what the numbers on the right mean? One request to the developer, could there could be an indication when you score a point, maybe a noise or thumbs up on the display or something? Obviously a full sized competitor avatar would be better than just a head but that's asking a bit much and doesn't really affect the play. Will use regularly to get a workout. One of the best games in my library so far and great for a workout.

developer_response.body: Thanks very much for your suggestions.

reviews.review_description: I haven't played the real badminton in years. So when I found this app my heart jumped with joy and the game did not disappoint.  The game play is very realistic and pass through helps a lot. Since I got it I played almost every day .

It’s such a good game I feel like real. Right now I just play solo with AI and haven’t tried the match. I haven’t figured out how to start. When I open the apps the game already start       Even play AI. I still prefer has some score. Highly recommended.
